site.name: Drop
site.tagline: Rally the group. Surprise the one.
site.description: Drop is the easiest way to coordinate a surprise Venmo flood for a friend or family member going through it. Everyone pledges in private, then sends at the same moment on drop day — so the recipient just experiences a wave of love hitting their phone, with no idea it was organized.
site.shortDescription: Coordinate a surprise Venmo flood for someone you love. They never see it coming.

pages.title: Drop — Rally the group. Surprise the one.
pages.description: Coordinate a surprise Venmo flood for someone you love. Everyone pledges in private, sends at the same moment on drop day. The recipient never sees it coming.
pages.h1: Rally the group. Surprise the one.

pages.title: How Drop works — surprise Venmo coordination in 4 steps
pages.description: Start a private drop, invite the crew, set drop day, and on the morning of, everyone hits send on Venmo at the same time. Here's the whole flow.

pages.title: Why Drop isn't a GoFundMe — and when each one is right
pages.description: GoFundMe is public, slow, and kills the surprise. Drop is private, peer-to-peer Venmo, and built so the recipient never knows it was organized.

pages.title: When to use Drop — real moments people have rallied for
pages.description: Medical bills, layoffs, basement floods, a friend who would never ask. Drop is for the moments a GoFundMe would feel wrong.

pages.title: Privacy — the recipient never sees Drop
pages.description: Drop never contacts the recipient. No account, no notification, no data. Contributor links are unguessable. The surprise is the whole product.

schema.name: Drop
schema.description: Coordinate a surprise Venmo flood for a friend or family member going through it. Private pledges, simultaneous drop day, recipient never knows.

faq.q: Does the recipient know about Drop?
faq.a: No. The recipient never has an account, never gets an email from Drop, and never sees the app. The surprise is the whole point.
No. Money flows directly Venmo-to-Venmo from each contributor to the recipient. We never touch the funds.

faq.q: What if someone doesn't send their pledge on drop day?
faq.a: Pledges are honor-system, just like a group chat. The organizer can nudge anyone whose pledge hasn't landed.

faq.q: How is this different from a GoFundMe?
faq.a: GoFundMe is public, takes a cut, and shows the recipient the running total. Drop is private, peer-to-peer, and engineered so the recipient experiences a spontaneous-feeling wave of Venmos with no idea it was coordinated.
